name: Jordan Whittington
headline: Buried behind Nacua and Adams, a deep stash only
body:
The 2025 tape underlines a player still fighting for snaps: 40.8% snap share across 15 games, a 36% route rate, and just 25 targets for a 4.3% target share. He turned that sliver into 18 catches, 171 yards and no scores, with a 0.77 yards-per-route mark and a 0.087 WOPR that scream tertiary option. His 0.103 EPA per play and 52% success rate were efficient enough, but the volume ceiling is the story, and his 2024 sample (2.42 YPRR, 0.59 EPA) shows the upside is real only if targets ever arrive.

That unlock is unlikely. Slotted third in line behind Puka Nacua and Davante Adams, he projects for roughly 15 grabs and 172 yards, leaving him near WR126 and off the draft board at a 301 ADP. He is a contingency play, not a roster asset, worth a flier only if injury thins the room ahead of him.
